lines: At the top of the table, Butters and Carter are locked together on 34pts — a tense, joyless marriage of equals, both desperately hoping the other slips up first.

Dene, meanwhile, is sitting on 25pts with just France left standing after Senegal, Bosnia, and Tunisia all packed their bags and went home — mate, you didn't enter a sweepstake, you entered a charity raffle and lost.

Ellis is propping up the table on 23pts with Argentina his sole survivor — one team, one dream, and the lingering shame of drawing Paraguay and New Zealand like a man who picks the worst Kinder Egg every single time.

Norway are the sweepstake's great unsung heroes, still alive and quietly making Al, Butters, Callum, Croft, and Wilmot all feel slightly better about themselves — five owners, one horse, absolute chaos if they go deep.

Today's only fixture is South Africa vs Canada — neither owned by anyone in the sweepstake, so feel free to watch it with the detached serenity of a man who has absolutely nothing to lose and nothing to gain.
